REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF DAMIEN QUINN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PER CRC 3.1110(A) "A NOTICE OF MOTION MUST STATE IN THE OPENING PARAGRAPH THE NATURE OF THE ORDER BEING SOUGHT AND THE GROUNDS FOR ISSUANCE OF THE ORDER." THE ONLY STATED GROUNDS FOR THE MOTION TO STRIKE IN THE OPENING PARAGRAPH OF THE NOTICE OF MOTION TO STRIKE IS "IRRELEVANT." (NOTICE OF MOTION 1:27). ALL OTHER PURPORTED GROUNDS FOR THE MOTION ARE NOT PROPERLY BEFORE THE COURT AS THEY ARE NOT PROPERLY NOTICED. IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND IN THE MOTION THE "IRRELEVANT" PARAGRAPHS ARE IDENTIFIED AS 84, 99, 121, 166, 177, AND 189. NO AUTHORITY IS CITED ADDRESSING IRRELEVANCY OF EACH PARTICULAR PARAGRAPH. NO ARGUMENT IS PROVIDED AS TO EACH PARTICULAR PARAGRAPH. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT JOHN KAUFMAN'S MOTION FOR ADMISSIONS AND GENUINENESS OF DOCUMENTS BE DEEMED ADMITTED. THE COURT FINDS AS TO THE RFAS THAT THE MOTION IS MOOT GIVEN THAT A VERIFICATION HAS NOW BEEN PROVIDED. HOWEVER, IT APPEARS THAT THE VERIFICATION WAS NOT PROVIDED BEFORE THE MOTION WAS FILED. IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER THE VERIFICATION PROVIDED TO THE RFAS ALSO APPLIES TO THE REQUESTS TO ADMIT GENUINENESS OF DOCUMENTS. PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDED AN AMENDED VERIFICATION BY OCTOBER 26, 2018. THE COURT AWARDS DEFENDANTS $450 IN SANCTIONS. SANCTIONS TO BE PAID BY NOVEMBER 2, 2018. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring Case To Trial Within Five Years Pursuant To Ccp 583.310
Off Calendar Sep-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BELROSE, LLC'S AND JOHN D. KAUFMAN'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO BRING CASE TO TRIAL WITHIN FIVE YEARS PURSUANT TO CCP 583.310.

Appearances via Zoom: Frank Bazadier (323.815.9339) for plaintiff and moving party; A.K. Abraham of the Abraham Law Offices (650.400.7812) for defendants and moving parties; No other appearances.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Late-filed opposition is considered and stricken. It is undisputed that the matter was not brought to trial within 5 years and 6 months. It is also undisputed that the parties did not stipulate to an extension. Plaintiff presents no arguments or evidence that bringing the complaint to trial was impractical or futile. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On Verified Fifth Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-03-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (via Zoom):

Denae Hildebrand Budde; Budde Law Group, APLC; 925-939-9880; appearing on behalf of cross-defendant Old Republic Title Company.
A.K. Abraham; Abraham Law Offices; 650-400-7812; appearing on behalf of defendants/cross-complainants Belrose LLC and John D. Kaufman.

Cousel for cross-defendant and moving party Old Republic Title Company contests the court's ruling as it pertains to defendant/cross-complainant Kaufman. The court explains its ruling on cross-defendant's objections to cross-complainant's evidence, which were overruled with the exception of objection number 12. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON VERIFIED FIFTH AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is DENIED. Cross-Defendant's Objections to Cross-Complainant's evidence are overruled, except objection number 12, which is sustained on hearsay grounds. Motion is DENIED as to Belrose, LLC. Motion for summary judgment is framed by the pleadings. Moving party fails to show that the operative complaint asserts any claims at issue in this motion by Belrose, LLC against the moving party. No judgment may be entered on claims not asserted in the complaint. Prevailing party to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). Motion is DENIED as to Kaufman. Triable issues of fact exist as to knowledge, breach of duty and good faith. Prevailing party to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT DAMIEN QUINN's MOTION TO STRIKE 5TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party appears to base its motion to strike on demurrer-like arguments (failure to attach a contract; failure to allege fraud with specificity). These are not proper grounds for the motion to strike. Furthermore, in the event allegations in the complaint are contradicted by the exhibits attached thereto, the exhibits control. There is no basis for striking the allegations. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
(Court call)
Steven Booska, 415.397.4345 for Cross-Defendant Andrew Chiu.
(Zoom)
Alexander Abraham, 650.348.7700 for Cross-Complaintants Bellrose LLC and John Kaufman.
Rose Njugu, 925.939.9880 for Cross-Defendant Old Republic Title Company.
Michael Woods, 415.759.19000 for Cross-Defendant Patrick Spiers.
Damien Quinn, 415.710.9323 Plaintiff.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY's DEMURRER to 5TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for negligence is sustained without leave to amend. Demurrer to the causes of action for breach of contract and breach of fiduciary duty is overruled. For the purposes of the demurrer, cross-complainant's feasible interpretation of the contract must be accepted. Paragraph 4.4. of the Escrow instructions suggests that "Capital Funding" and "undersigned" are not one and the same as is urged by the moving party. "Your written request for funds specifying the anticipated business day of recording shall be communicated to the undersigned and Capital Funding." Ms. Njugu to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Rose Njugu, Esq. of Budde Law Group, 925-939-9880, appearing for Old Republic Title Company, Cross Defendant and moving party.

No other appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND.

As to the Seventh COA: Cross-claimant shall allege facts showing the formation and operation of a conspiracy among the alleged co-conspirators. Since fraud is alleged to be the object of the conspiracy, the claim must be pleaded with particularity and cross-claimant must allege facts showing "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the misrepresentations were tendered. As to the Eighth COA: Cross-claimant must plead facts of ORTC's role in the alleged enterprise and the conduct involving the element of direction. As to the allegations of mail fraud, Cross-claimant must also detail with particularity the time, place, and manner of each act of fraud, plus the role of each defendant in each scheme. As to the allegations of extortion, Cross-claimant must plead facts showing ORTC or any other cross-defendant "obtained" property by actual or threatened force.As to the fourteenth COA: Cross-claimant should specifically plead the business practice and conduct that is alleged to be 'unfair' under Business & Professions Code section 17200 as it pertains to ORTC (order for the same signed in open court) Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike To First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF DAMIEN QUINN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike To First Amended Complaint is DENIED. Per CRC 3.1110(a) "A notice of motion must state in the opening paragraph the nature of the order being sought and the grounds for issuance of the order." The only stated grounds for the Motion to Strike in the opening paragraph of the Notice of Motion to Strike is "irrelevant." (Notice of Motion 1:27). All other purported grounds for the Motion are not properly before the Court as they are not properly noticed.
In the Points and Authorities and in the Motion the "irrelevant" paragraphs are identified as 84, 99, 121, 166, 177, and 189. No authority is cited addressing irrelevancy of each particular paragraph. No argument is provided as to each particular paragraph. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
